layout_dets.text: Reliability and validity

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Relationship to use

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Regression analyses (Table 9) indicate that the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effect of usefulness on usage, controlling for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ease of use, was significant at the .001 level

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for Chart-Master (b = .69), Pendraw (b = .76) and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: overall (b=.75). In contrast, the effect of ease

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of use on usage, controlling for usefulness, was

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: questions were worded as follows: "Assuming

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Pendraw would be available on my job, I predict

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that I will use it on a regular basis in the future,"

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: followed by two seven-point scales, one with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: likely-unlikely end-point adjectives, the other, re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: versed in polarity, with improbable-probable end-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: point adjectives. Such self-predictions, or "be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havioral expectations," are among the most ac-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: curate predictors available for an individual's

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: future behavior (Sheppard, et al., 1988; War-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: shaw and Davis, 1985). For Chart-Master, use-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fulness was significantly correlated with self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: predicted usage (r=.71, p<.001), but ease of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use was not (r=.25, n.s.) (Table 8). Chart-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Master had a non-significant correlation between

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ease of use and usefulness (r= .25, n.s.). For

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Pendraw, usage was significantly correlated with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: both usefulness (r= .59, p<.001) and ease of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use (r=.47, p<.001). The ease of use-useful-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ness correlation was significiant for Pendraw

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (r=.38, p<.001). When data were pooled across

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: systems, usage correlated .85 (p<.001) with use-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fulness and .59 (p<.001) with ease of use (see

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Table 8). Ease of use correlated with usefulness

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: .56 (p<.001). The overall usefulness-use corre-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lation was significantly greater than the ease of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use-use correlation, as indicated by a test of de-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pendent correlations (t77= 4.78, p<.001) (Cohen

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Cohen, 1975).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Participants were asked to self-predict their

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: future use of Chart-Master and Pendraw. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Table 7. Factor Analysis of Perceived Usefulness

layout_dets.text: and Ease of Use Items: Study 2

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Cronbach alpha was .98 for perceived useful-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ness and .94 for perceived ease of use. Con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: vergent validity was supported, with only two of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 72 monotrait-heteromethod correlations falling

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: below significance. Ease of use item 4 (flexibil-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ity), applied to Chart-Master, was not significantly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: correlated with either items 3 (clear and under-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: standable) or 5 (easy to become skillful). This

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: suggests that, contrary to conventional wisdom,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: flexibility is not always associated with ease of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use. As Goodwin (1987) points out, flexibility can

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: actually impair ease of use, particularly for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: novice users. With item 4 omitted, Cronbach

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: alpha for ease of use would increase from .94

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to .95. Despite the two departures to conver-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: gent validity related to ease of use item 4, no

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: exceptions to the discriminant validity criteria oc-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: curred across a total of 720 comparisons (360

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for each scale).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Factorial validity was assessed by factor ana-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lyzing the 12 scale items using principal compo-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nents extraction and oblique rotation. The re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sulting two-factor solution is very consistent with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: distinct, unidimensional usefulness and each of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use scales (Table 7). Thus, as in Study 1, Study

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2 reflects favorably on the convergent, discrimi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nant, and factorial validity of the usefulness and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ease of use scales.

layout_dets.text: The purpose of this investigation was to develop

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and validate new measurement scales for per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ceived usefulness and perceived ease of use,

layout_dets.text: two distinct variables hypothesized to be deter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: indicates that the variance in usage explained

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by ease of use drops by 91% when usefulness

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is controlled for. Consistent with Study 1, these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: regression and partial correlation results suggest

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that usefulness mediates the effect of ease of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use on usage. The implications of this are ad-

layout_dets.text: The new scales exhibited excellent psychomet-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ric characteristics. Convergent and discriminant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: validity were strongly supported by multitrait-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: multimethod analyses in both validation studies.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: These two data sets also provided strong sup-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: port for factorial validity: the pattern of factor load-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ings confirmed that a priori structure of the two

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: instruments, with usefulness items loading highly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: on one factor, ease of use items loading highly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: on the other factor, and small cross-factor load-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ings. Cronbach alpha reliability for perceived use-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fulness was .97 in Study 1 and .98 in Study 2.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Reliability for ease of use was .91 in Study 1

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and .94 in Study 2. These findings mutually con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: firm the psychometric strength of the new meas-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: urement scales.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: minants of computer usage. This effort was suc-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cessful in several respects. The new scales were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: found to have strong psychometric properties

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and to exhibit significant empirical relationships

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with self-reported measures of usage behavior.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Also, several new insights were generated about

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the nature of perceived usefulness and ease of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use, and their roles as determinants of user

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acceptance.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The new scales were developed, refined, and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: streamlined in a several-step process. Explicit

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: definitions were stated, followed by a theoretical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: analysis from a variety of perspectives, includ-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing: expectancy theory; self-efficacy theory; be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havioral decision theory; diffusion of innovations;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: marketing; and human-computer interaction, re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: garding why usefulness and ease of use are hy-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pothesized as important determinants of system

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use. Based on the stated definitions, initial scale

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: items were generated. To enhance content va-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lidity, these were pretested in a small pilot study,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and several items were eliminated. The remain-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing items, 10 for each of the two constructs, were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tested for validity and reliability in Study 1, a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: field study of 112 users and two systems (the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: PROFS electronic mail system and the XEDIT

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: file editor). Item analysis was performed to elimi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nate more items and refine others, further stream-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lining and purifying the scales. The resulting six-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: item scales were subjected to further construct

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: validation in Study 2, a lab study of 40 users

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and two systems: Chart-Master (a menu-driven

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: business charting program) and Pendraw (a bit-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mapped paint program with a digitizer tablet as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: its input device).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: One of the most significant findings is the rela-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tive strength of the usefulness-usage relation-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ship compared to the ease of use-usage rela-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tionship. In both studies, usefulness was

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: significantly more strongly linked to usage than

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was ease of use. Examining the joint direct effect

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of the two variables on use in regression analy-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ses, this difference was even more pronounced:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the usefulness-usage relationship remained

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: large, while the ease of use-usage relationship

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was diminished substantially (Table 8). Multi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: collinearity has been ruled out as an explana-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion for the results using specific tests for the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: presence of multicollinearity. In hindsight, the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: prominence of perceived usefulness makes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sense conceptually: users are driven to adopt

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: an application primarily because of the functions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: it performs for them, and secondarily for how

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: easy or hard it is to get the system to perform

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: those functions. For instance, users are often

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: willing to cope with some difficulty of use in a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: system that provides critically needed function-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ality. Although difficulty of use can discourage

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: adoption of an otherwise useful system, no

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: amount of ease of use can compensate for a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: IT Usefulness and Ease of Use

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Research implications

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: This investigation has limitations that should be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pointed out. The generality of the findings re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mains to be shown by future research. The fact

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that similar findings were observed, with respect

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to both the psychometric properties of the meas-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ures and the pattern of empirical associations,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: across two different user populations, two differ-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ent systems, and two different research settings

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (lab and field), provides some evidence favoring

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: external validity.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: From a causal perspective, the regression re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sults suggest that ease of use may be an ante-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cedent to usefulness, rather than a parallel,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: direct determinant of usage. The significant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pairwise correlation between ease of use and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: usage all but vanishes when usefulness is con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: trolled for. This, coupled with a significant ease

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of use-usefulness correlation is exactly the pat-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tern one would expect if usefulness mediated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: between ease of use and usage (e.g., J.A.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Davis, 1985). That is, the results are consistent

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with an ease of use --> usefulness --> usage

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: chain of causality. These results held both for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pooled observations and for each individual

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: system (Table 8). The causal influence of ease

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of use on usefulness makes sense conceptu-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ally, too. All else being equal, the easier a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: system is to interact with, the less effort needed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to operate it, and the more effort one can allo-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cate to other activities (Radner and Rothschild,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1975), contributing to overall job performance.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Goodwin (1987) also argues for this flow of cau-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sality, concluding from her analysis that: "There

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is increasing evidence that the effective func-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tionality of a system depends on its usability"

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (p. 229). This intriguing interpretation is prelimi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nary and should be subjected to further experi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mentation. If true, however, it underscores the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: theoretical importance of perceived usefulness.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In addition, a follow-up to this study, reported

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by Davis, et al. (1989) found a very similar pat-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly/September 1989

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Further research will shed more light on the gen-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: erality of these findings. Another limitation is that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the usage measures employed were self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reported as opposed to objectively measured.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Not enough is currently known about how accu-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: rately self-reports reflect actual behavior. Also,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: since usage was reported on the same ques-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tionnaire used to measure usefulness and ease

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of use, the possibility of a halo effect should not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: be overlooked. Future research addressing the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relationship between these constructs and ob-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: jectively measured use is needed before claims

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: about the behavioral predictiveness can be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: made conclusively. These limitations notwithstand-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing, the results represent a promising step

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: toward the establishment of improved measures

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for two important variables.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: system that does not perform a useful function.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The prominence of usefulness over ease of use

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: has important implications for designers, particu-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: larly in the human factors tradition, who have

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tended to overemphasize ease of use and over-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: look usefulness (e.g., Branscomb and Thomas,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1984; Chin, et al., 1988; Shneiderman, 1987).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Thus, a major conclusion of this study is that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perceived usefulness is a strong correlate of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: user acceptance and should not be ignored by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: those attempting to design or implement suc-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cessful systems.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Future research is needed to address how other

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: variables relate to usefulness, ease of use, and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acceptance. Intrinsic motivation, for example,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: has received inadequate attention in MIS theo-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ries. Whereas perceived usefulness is con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cerned with performance as a consequence use,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: intrinsic motivation is concerned with the rein-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: forcement and enjoyment related to the process

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of performing a behavior per se, irrespective of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: whatever external outcomes are generated by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: such behavior (Deci, 1975). Although intrinsic

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: motivation has been studied in the design of com-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: puter games (e.g., Malone, 1981), it is just be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ginning to be recognized as a potential mecha-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nism underlying user acceptance of end-user

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tern of results in a two-wave study (Tables 8

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and 9). In that study, MBA student subjects were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: asked to fill out a questionnaire after a one-hour

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: introduction to a word processing program, and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: again 14 weeks later. Usage intentions were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: measured at both time periods, and self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reported usage was measured at the later time

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: period. Intentions were significantly correlated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with usage (.35 and .63 for the two points in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: time, respectively). Unlike the results of Studies

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1 and 2, Davis, et al. (1989) found a significant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: direct effect of ease of use on usage, controlling

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for usefulness, after the one-hour training ses-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sion (Table 9), although this evolved into a non-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: significant effect as of 14 weeks later. In gen-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: eral, though, Davis, et al. (1989) found useful-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ness to be more influential than ease of use in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: driving usage behavior, consistent with the find-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ings reported above.

layout_dets.text: Acknowledgements

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: More research is needed to understand how

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: measures such as those introduced here per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: form in applied design and evaluation settings.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The growing literature on design principles (An-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: derson and Olson, 1985; Gould and Lewis,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1985; Johansen and Baker, 1984; Mantei and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Teorey, 1988; Shneiderman, 1987) calls for the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use of subjective measures at various points

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: throughout the development and implementation

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: process, from the earliest needs assessment

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Although there has been a growing pessimism

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in the field about the ability to identify measures

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that are robustly linked to user acceptance, the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: view taken here is much more optimistic. User

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reactions to computers are complex and multi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: faceted. But if the field continues to systemati-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cally investigate fundamental mechanisms driv-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing user behavior, cultivating better and better

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: measures and critically examining alternative theo-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: retical models, sustainable progress is within

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reach.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: School. The author is indebted to the anony-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mous associate editor and reviewers for their

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: many helpful suggestions.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Abelson, R.P. and Levi, A. "Decision Making and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Decision Theory," in The Handbook of Social

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Psychology, third edition, G. Lindsay and E.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Aronson (eds.), Knopf, New York, NY, 1985,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly/September 1989

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: systems (Carroll and Thomas, 1988). Currently,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the role of affective attitudes is also an open

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: issue. While some theorists argue that beliefs

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influence behavior only via their indirect influ-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ence on attitudes (e.g., Fishbein and Ajzen,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1975), others view beliefs and attitudes as co-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: determinants of behavioral intentions (e.g., Tri-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: andis, 1977), and still others view attitudes as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: antecedents of beliefs (e.g., Weiner, 1986).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Counter to Fishbein and Ajzen's (1975) position,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: both Davis (1986) and Davis, et al. (1989) found

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that attitudes do not fully mediate the effect of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perceived usefulness and perceived ease of use

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: on behavior.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: through concept screening and prototype test-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing to post-implementation assessment. The fact

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that the measures performed well psychometri-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cally both after brief introductions to the target

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: system (Study 2, and Davis, et al., 1989) and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: after substantial user experience with the system

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Study 1, and Davis, et al., 1989) is promising

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: concerning their appropriateness at various

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: points in the life cycle. Practitioners generally

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: evaluate systems not only to predict acceptabil-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ity but also to diagnose the reasons underlying

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lack of acceptance and to formulate interven-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tions to improve user acceptance. In this sense,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research on how usefulness and ease of use

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: can be influenced by various externally control-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lable factors, such as the functional and inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: face characteristics of the system (Benbasat and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Dexter, 1986; Bewley, et al., 1983; Dickson, et

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: al., 1986), development methodologies (Alavi,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1984), training and education (Nelson and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Cheney, 1987), and user involvement in design

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Baroudi, et al. 1986; Franz and Robey, 1986)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is important. The new measures introduced here

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: can be used by researchers investigating these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: issues.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: References

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: It should be emphasized that perceived useful-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ness and ease of use are people's subjective

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: appraisal of performance and effort, respectively,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and do not necessarily reflect objective reality.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In this study, beliefs are seen as meaningful vari-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ables in their own right, which function as be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havioral determinants, and are not regarded as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: surrogate measures of objective phenomena (as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is often done in MIS research, e.g., Ives, et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1983; Srinivasan, 1985). Several MIS studies

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have observed discrepancies between perceived

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and actual performance (Cats-Baril and Huber,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1987; Dickson, et al., 1986; Gallupe and De-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Sanctis, 1988; Mclntyre, 1982; Sharda, et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1988). Thus, even if an application would objec-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tively improve performance, if users don't per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ceive it as useful, they're unlikely to use it (Alavi

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Henderson, 1981). Conversely, people may

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: overrate the performance gains a system has

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to offer and adopt systems that are dysfunc-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tional. Given that this study indicates that people

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: act according to their beliefs about performance,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: future research is needed to understand why per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formance beliefs are often in disagreement with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: objective reality. The possibility of dysfunctional

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: impacts generated by information technology

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (e.g., Kottemann and Remus, 1987) emphasizes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that user acceptance is not a universal goal and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is actually undesireable in cases where systems

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fail to provide true performance gains.
